title: The bureaucracy: functions and responsibilities: foundational
description: Test your knowledge of the largest workforce in the federal government!
topic_path: The bureaucracy: functions and responsibilities: foundational

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is one main responsibility of the bureaucracy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: providing information about a certain industry to Congress
passing bills that interest groups have lobbied for

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is one of the bureaucracy's main responsibilities?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: processing and issuing verdicts on legal cases
passing laws that affect the daily functions of the government

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following tasks does the bureaucracy carry out?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What is one power that belongs to the bureaucracy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What is one of the duties of the bureaucracy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Sometimes,  a group of individuals, public officials, and interest groups come together to promote a particular cause.

**What’s the name for this type of group?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Congressional committees, interest groups, and the bureaucracy sometimes work together to achieve something that benefits all of them.

**What’s the name for this relationship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

An iron triangle is made up of three groups. One of the groups approves higher budget requests for government agencies, supports legislation that benefits particular interest groups, and receives contributions and information in return. 

**Which part of the iron triangle is this?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

One part of an iron triangle gives the government information about a certain issue. It also encourages lawmakers to draft legislation to help its cause, sometimes through making campaign donations to congress members. 

**Which part of the iron triangle is this?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which part of an iron triangle gathers information, oversees regulations, and relies on Congress for its funding?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What is political patronage?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the practice of hiring and promoting someone because of their politics
the practice of hiring employees based on their qualifications
the relationship between Congress, government agencies, and interest groups
the day-to-day operations of administering the government

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following describes the practice of hiring individuals as a political favor?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What is the civil service?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the permanent, administrative, nonpartisan part of the federal government
all elected and appointed government officials
all non-governmental organizations, like charities and interest groups
the network of all active-duty military officials across the United States

assessment_items.question_content:
**What did the Pendleton Civil Service Act of 1883 establish?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Civil servants will be hired and promoted based on merit instead of political patronage.
Government workers may be hired on their political affiliation instead of their qualifications.
Interest groups have the right to lobby members of Congress to forward the group's agenda.
Civil servants must reapply for work whenever a new administration takes office.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What model is used to hire and promote workers based on their qualifications and performance?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What is another term for the permanent workforce of the federal bureaucracy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What is an executive agency?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: an independent group that deals with specific government programs
a business that the government operates by hiring civil servants
an independent organization that makes rules that businesses must follow
a group of bureaucratic leaders who are appointed by the president

assessment_items.question_content:
The president's cabinet includes the vice president and 15 government leaders. 

**What sort of governmental bodies do these 15 people oversee?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following is the name for a business that the government owns and operates?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following describes a government agency that makes and enforces policies in order to protect the people?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
